name: Queen of Sheba Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Prepare two 9-inch springform pans by covering their bottoms with foil and attaching the sides.
2. Finely grind almonds with 4 tablespoons of sugar. Sift flour, cocoa, and baking powder into a small bowl.
3. Stir in the almond mixture.
4. Beat egg yolks and 8 tablespoons of sugar using an electric mixer until ribbon-like strands form when the beaters are lifted (about 6 minutes).
5. In another bowl, beat egg whites with cream of tartar until stiff but not dry.
6. Gradually add the remaining 4 tablespoons of sugar and beat until stiff and shiny.
7. Fold one-third of the almond/cocoa mixture into the yolks, then one-third of the egg whites. Repeat twice.
8. Spoon half of the batter into each pan, smoothing the surface with a spatula.
9. Bake for about 20 minutes or until a tester inserted in the center comes out dry. Cool in the pans for 5 minutes.
10. Invert the cakes onto racks and cool completely.
11. Melt chocolate over hot (but not boiling) water in a double boiler. Bring milk to simmer in a heavy saucepan over low heat.
12. Whisk egg yolks and sugar in a bowl until smooth. Gradually whisk in the hot milk, return to the saucepan, and stir over low heat until the mixture leaves a path on the back of a spoon when your finger is drawn across it (about 5 to 7 minutes). Do not boil.
13. Gradually whisk the custard into the chocolate until smooth. Cool completely and stir occasionally.
14. Whip cream to soft peaks. Fold into the cooled chocolate mixture in three batches.
15. Assemble by cutting each cake into two layers using a serrated knife. Set one layer, cut side up, in the springform pan. Brush 4 tablespoons of the parfait onto the cake in the pan.
16. Spoon and spread 1/4 of the chocolate parfait over the cake, reaching the edge. Top with the second layer, cut side up. Repeat with the remaining parfait and liqueur.
17. Add the third and fourth layers, repeating with the liqueur and parfait until smooth. Smooth the top of the parfait with a spatula.
18. Cover and freeze for at least 4 hours.
19. Remove pan sides. Refrigerate the cake while preparing the cream.
20. Melt chocolate in a double boiler over hot (but not boiling) water. Stir until smooth.
21. Whip cream with sugar and vanilla to stiff peaks. Whisk in the chocolate.
22. Spoon the mixture into a pastry bag fitted with a medium star tip. Set the cake on a serving platter.
23. Pipe decorative whipped cream rosettes around the edge of the cake. Place almonds or optional chocolate leaves/curls as garnish.
